text:
Market today: The market shows a mixed sentiment. While general stocks may rally with Congress on break, your tech-heavy Nasdaq-100 names face increased volatility and a cautionary signal from Microsoft's job cuts. This directly impacts your core holdings.

Your holdings: SOXL is a primary concern, flagged 'SELL' and down 23.52% on a leveraged product. SPAL, also leveraged and new, is down 11.17% and nearing its stop. Remember, margin amplifies these drops.

Watch: Immediately review SOXL's position, as its original stop is now above the current price. Keep a close eye on SPAL as it approaches its stop. Monitor overall tech sector sentiment after Microsoft's news.

Bottom line: Prioritize aggressive risk management on your leveraged, underperforming tech positions.
plays: Trim SOXL: It's flagged 'SELL' and down 23.52% on a leveraged product. Re-evaluate your stop and consider reducing exposure.
Watch SPAL closely: This new, leveraged ETF is down 11.17% and approaching its 21.38 stop. Be ready to act if it breaks.
Consider LRCX for a new buy: Scanner shows 'BUY' 9/12, entry 349.85-349.23, stop 347.77. It's a chip name with good bias and value.
